mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 6.0 percent indicates persistent labor market slack that suppresses consumer spending and corporate earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) when unemployment remains above 6.0% for more than two consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts signal broad-based consumer demand expansion that directly boosts corporate revenues, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when either series declines, or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing output and capacity utilization that translates into higher short-term corporate cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during inflationary supply bottlenecks, or 5-day forward returns show no significant correlation (Rank IC < 0.01) with INDPRO momentum.

mechanism_narrative: A consumer price index above 235.0 signals accelerating inflation that forces markets to price in earlier monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >50% of observations when CPIAUCSL exceeds 235.0, or the threshold effect vanishes in a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market tightening that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The combined signal loses predictive accuracy when payroll growth falls below 150,000 while unemployment declines, or the interaction effect yields a negative out-of-sample Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: Positive quarter-over-quarter real GDP growth reflects expanding economic output that directly increases corporate profit margins and dividend capacity, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >55% of quarters with positive GDP growth, or the rolling Rank IC remains below 0.02 across all tested windows.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production exceeding 105.0 indicates capacity utilization approaching physical limits that trigger input cost inflation and supply chain bottlenecks, compressing corporate margins and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns when commodity prices are stable, or forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered instances over a 5-year walk-forward test.
